---
title: "LRP56: Mechanism model — receptive vocabulary (R) -> word reading (W)"

date: last-modified
date-format: "YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss Z"

format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    number-sections: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-links:
      - repo
---

::: {.callout-warning title="Warning: Preliminary model output"}

This model output is preliminary and likely to change as the model evolves and further data are received.

:::

```{python}
# | echo: false
import json
from pathlib import Path

import arviz as az
import pandas as pd

_here = Path(".")
with open(_here / "config.json") as f:
    config = json.load(f)
trace = az.from_netcdf(_here / "trace.nc")
```

## Overview

LRP56 targets the causal effect $f^R$ of receptive vocabulary on word reading
using *all three phase transitions* (t1$\to$t2, t2$\to$t3, t3$\to$t4) with
phase-specific intercepts. The mechanism variable enters as a 1-D HSGP on
$\text{logit}_*(R_{\text{post}}, N_R)$.

## Adjustment set

Backdoor paths from $R_{\text{post}}$ to $W_{\text{post}}$ are blocked by the
DAG-derived adjustment set:

- $G$ — $G \to R$ and $G \to W$ (linear, one coefficient $\beta_G$).
- $A$ — $A \to R$ and $A \to W$. The age-GP flag is **off by default**
  (see `notes/202604181700-lrp55-age-gp-drop.md`); re-enable via
  `ModelSpec.extra={"use_age_gp": True}` for sensitivity.
- $W_{\text{pre}}$ — baseline word reading (temporal precedence; not a collider).

$E$ is a *descendant* of $R$ (path $R \to E$) that also affects $W$.
Conditioning on $E$ would block the legitimate indirect path $R \to E \to W$
and bias $f^R$ toward zero. $E$ is therefore **not** in the adjustment set.

### Identification caveats

- *No unmeasured confounder assumption* (NUCA) for $(R_{\text{post}}, W_{\text{post}})$: strong and not testable.
- $R_{\text{post}}$ is measured with error; if measurement error is non-negligible, $f^R$ attenuates toward zero (a latent-$R$ extension is out of scope here).
- Across-phase pooling assumes the structural relationship is stable over time; a phase-specific sensitivity fit is available via the `phase_specific_mechanism` flag.

![Model diagram](model_graph.svg){#fig-model .lightbox fig-align="left"}

## Shared priors

![alpha](prior_alpha.svg){width=40%}
![tau](prior_tau.svg){width=40%}
![gamma_own](prior_gamma_own.svg){width=40%}
![gamma_cross](prior_gamma_cross.svg){width=40%}
![kappa](prior_kappa.svg){width=40%}

## Sampling diagnostics

```{python}
# | echo: false
diag = pd.read_csv(_here / "diagnostics.csv", index_col=0)
diag
```

![Trace](trace_plot.png){.lightbox}
![Energy](energy_plot.png){.lightbox}

## Mechanism curve

![Mechanism curve](mechanism_curve.png){.lightbox}

```{python}
# | echo: false
mech = pd.read_csv(_here / "mechanism_curve.csv")
mech.head(15)
```

The marginal slope of $f^R$ at the sample mean of $\text{logit}_*(R_{\text{post}})$
is a single-number summary; it can be computed from the posterior draws as the
centred-difference derivative over the fitted grid.

## Posterior predictive

![Posterior predictive check](posterior_predictive_check.png){.lightbox}

## LOO-PSIS

```{python}
# | echo: false
# | output: asis
print("```\n" + open(_here / "loo.txt").read() + "\n```")
```

## Configuration

```{python}
# | echo: false
config
```
